chunk_summary: Management of growth faltering in infants under six months focuses on skilled breastfeeding assessment, maternal health screening, and facility-based care for severe cases, emphasizing exclusive breastfeeding and dyad-centered interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of breastfeeding assessment in managing growth faltering in infants under six months.
Identify maternal health factors that impact infant feeding and growth.
Learn facility-based care protocols for infants with severe malnutrition or danger signs.
Explore strategies for catch-up feeding when exclusive breastfeeding cannot be established.
key_facts: Growth faltering in infants under six months is managed as a mother–infant dyad problem.
Anthropometric screening, such as weight-for-age, is used to identify at-risk infants in primary care.
Danger signs like dehydration, hypothermia, or persistent vomiting require facility-based care.
Exclusive breastfeeding is prioritized through positioning correction and increased feeding frequency.
The MAA program framework supports breastfeeding promotion in India.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: The MAA (Mothers’ Absolute Affection) program framework supports breastfeeding promotion and maternal-infant care in India. (National Health Mission, Government of India, 2016)
document_key_topics: Management of growth faltering in infants under six months
Breastfeeding assessment and maternal health screening
